title: Legendary Hip Hop Artist Reveals UFO Obsession (Ft. Flying Lotus)
summary: Jesse Michels interviews musician and filmmaker Flying Lotus about their shared interest in UFOs and the broader UAP community. The conversation covers various UFO cases, whistleblowers, the challenges of the UFO community, Spielberg's possible insider knowledge, and the role of DMT in consciousness exploration.

acronyms.definition: Modern term for UFOs used by government and military
Digital visual effects used in film and video production
A powerful psychedelic compound that produces intense hallucinatory experiences

events.description: Back to the Future released, the same year Thomas Townsend Brown died
Year referenced in Back to the Future when Thomas Townsend Brown allegedly proved his experiment
Randy Anderson was allegedly taken to a deep underground military base at Naval Surface Warfare Crane in Indiana

key_claims.claim: Steven Spielberg told Ronald Reagan's White House screening audience that people in the room knew everything in ET was absolutely true
Eric Hecker claims there is a neutrino emitter and detector at the South Pole that was responsible for the Christchurch earthquake in New Zealand
Ross Coulthart claims there is a buried UFO that was too big to move and had to be built around
Close Encounters of the Third Kind contains crates labeled TRW and Skunk Works before those organizations were widely known
Back to the Future may contain encoded references to Thomas Townsend Brown's anti-gravity experiments
Spielberg said UFOs might be humans from 500,000 years in the future rather than extraterrestrials
Flying Lotus saw a being with three fingers and three toes, referring to the Peruvian tridactyl mummies
